name: Winter Tomato Garlic Macaroni Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons olive oil, butter, or butter/oil mix
26 ounces tomatoes, chopped (or 1 can plum tomatoes)
1 quart chicken broth or vegetable broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate garlic bulbs into cloves and peel.
2. Divide garlic cloves into three piles.
3. Mince onions with one third of the garlic cloves (use a food processor). Heat oil or butter in a large pot and sauté onion and garlic until soft but not brown.
4. Slice leeks thinly with another third of the garlic cloves (use a food processor or knife), and add to the pot. Sauté until soft.
5. Remove stems and seeds from ancho peppers.
6. Add tomatoes, broths, water, herbs, and ancho pepper to the pot. If desired, chop fresh herbs and dried ancho pepper in cheesecloth; tie up like a bag for easy removal later.
7. Bring to a boil, cover, lower heat, and simmer for two hours until onions and leeks are well melted into the soup.
8. If not using the herbs/pepper, remove them now. Slice reserved garlic thinly and add to the pot.
9. Add water if the soup is too thick; simmer covered for 30 minutes.
10. Before serving, uncover the pot, taste the soup, season with salt and pepper, and bring to a rolling boil.
11. Add macaroni (cook less than usual as it won't be drained) or cook macaroni separately and add just before serving.
12. Grate parmesan cheese over the top if desired.
Note: Tomato broth – For one quart of tomato broth, chop four pounds or more of fresh or canned plum tomatoes (or use 4 lbs of Pomi tomatoes in a box). Add 1 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon chopped basil, parsley, and rosemary. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally, then strain through a fine mesh strainer with a wire whisk.
